steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank falls between "motion" and "finding that elusive thread..." The phrase after the blank elaborates on what 'motion' means — it is not a new independent clause, it is an explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘motion, finding:’: Places a comma before "finding" and then a colon after it, making a fragment with nothing meaningful after the colon. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘motion. Finding’: A period creates two sentences, but "Finding that elusive thread..." is not an independent clause — it is a fragment. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘motion; finding’: A semicolon requires independent clauses on both sides. "Finding that elusive thread..." is not independent. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘motion: finding’: A colon introduces an elaboration of what precedes it. "Finding that elusive thread..." explains what the motion is — this is exactly what a colon does. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
